name: Kids OS
gate_blockers: COPPA compliance review required before any child data collection
Age-appropriate UX flows not yet specified (ages 4-7, 8-12, 13-17 are very different)
10 parent interviews needed to validate feature priority
Welfare flagging routing must be reviewed by a child safety professional
Misidentification screening framing requires legal and clinical review
Irlen screening tool UX not yet specified
assertions: Love of learning declines when self-worth is tied to performance
Evidence-based confidence (win logging) is more durable than praise-based confidence
Children who know their own learning patterns enter adulthood with a significant advantage
50% of misdiagnosed learning/attention issues have simpler explanations that can be screened for free
anti_drift_notes: Love of learning is the #1 metric — never let grades or performance scores override this
Self-worth is NEVER tied to performance — this is a hard design constraint
Welfare flags must NEVER route to parents who may be the source of harm
Hardship protocol is constitutional — children never lose access for financial reasons
Future Self Simulator uses child's own velocity, not population averages
Irlen screening and misidentification review are informational tools, not diagnostic — always refer to professionals after
Integrity Score is a mirror, not a judge — never punitive
Double points for catching yourself manipulating — this earns the highest recognition
decision_debt: Age segmentation UX — the 4-year-old and 15-year-old experience need completely different interfaces
Welfare flagging legal review — what is the platform's liability exposure for detecting but not acting?
Teacher OS integration model — how do teacher and parent permissions intersect?
Lumin University bridge — technical handoff of the full history record
Irlen screening implementation — digital overlay trial is novel; needs usability validation
Graduation diploma — what certifying body, what standards? Pre-accreditation path.
candidate_concepts: Peer connection matching (supervised, mission-aligned social layer)
Co-op family groups (multiple Kids OS families coordinating curriculum)
Special education adapter (IDEA-aligned features, IEP tracking)
Language learning track (natural acquisition approach, not grammar-first)
